pain_points: High operational overhead managing multiple observability tools requiring constant context-switching
No standardized performance benchmarking framework - ad-hoc testing with inconsistent tools and metrics across teams
Delayed insights from batch processing - day-old Hive data prevents real-time anomaly detection during migrations
No real-time correlation between cost and performance metrics
High infrastructure costs on legacy Intel hardware with expensive licensing, power consumption, and limited performance per dollar
Data scattered across disparate systems (Kafka, Splunk, Prometheus, Hive) with no unified view
Lack of visibility into workload characteristics by hardware platform - cannot identify migration candidates or quantify performance differences
use_cases: Application-Specific Migration Prioritization - identify which applications benefit most from AMD migration
Baseline Performance Comparison: Intel vs AMD - establish performance baselines across identical workloads on different architectures
Continuous Performance Regression Detection - automatically detect performance regressions after infrastructure changes
Stress Test & Capacity Planning - determine maximum sustainable load and breaking points under stress conditions
Consolidate Kafka & Hive Metrics into Unified Observability - bring fragmented data into Elasticsearch for real-time correlation
Cost-Performance Analysis & TCO Modeling - quantify cost savings and calculate ROI for Intel-to-AMD migration
customer_context.scale: Thousands of compute-intensive applications running on Intel Xeon and AMD EPYC processors
metrics: Error Rates & SLA Compliance (event.outcome failure percentage, SLA violations)
Data Ingestion Rates (events per second into Elasticsearch)
Cost Efficiency Calculations (cost per transaction, performance per dollar, TCO projections)
Kafka Metrics (consumer lag, message throughput, topic size)
Infrastructure Cost Attribution (hourly compute cost, power consumption, licensing costs)
Benchmark Scores (SPEC CPU 2017, TPC-H, Sysbench, Kafka performance)
CPU Utilization by Architecture (system.cpu.total.pct by host.cpu.model.name)
Custom Financial Workload Benchmarks (trade settlement rate, risk calculation throughput, payment validation latency)
Hive Query Performance (execution time, data scanned)
Disk I/O Performance (system.diskio.read/write.bytes, IOPS, latency percentiles)
